entries.definition:
كتاب الحاء

حلب

(حلف) الحاء واللام والفاء أصلٌ واحد، وهو الملازمة. يقال حالف فلانٌ

ḥ-l-f to be solid; an oath, to swear, to take an oath; to become an ally, to enter into an alliance, an alliance. Of this root, two forms occur 13 times in the Qurʾan: حَلَفَ ḥalafa 12 times and حَلَّاف ḥallāf once.
حَلَفَ ḥalafa i [v. intrans. with عَلَىٰ which is often elided] to swear, to take an oath (9:56) وَيَحْلِفُونَ بِاللَّهِ إِنَّهُمْ لَمِنكُمْ and they swear by God that they belong with you; *(58:14) يَحْلِفُونَ عَلَى الْكَذِبِ they swear to the truthfulness of a falsehood [lit. they swear on the lying].

حَلَّاف ḥallāf [intens. act. part.] one given to too much swearing, one who takes oaths too easily and too often (68:10) وَلَا تُطِعْ كُلَّ حَلَّافٍ مَّهِينٍ and do not yield to any contemptible swearer.
entries.definition_english:
ḥ-l-f to be solid; an oath, to swear, to take an oath; to become an ally, to enter into an alliance, an alliance. Of this root, two forms occur 13 times in the Qurʾan: حَلَفَ ḥalafa 12 times and حَلَّاف ḥallāf once.
حَلَفَ ḥalafa i [v. intrans. with عَلَىٰ which is often elided] to swear, to take an oath (9:56) وَيَحْلِفُونَ بِاللَّهِ إِنَّهُمْ لَمِنكُمْ and they swear by God that they belong with you; *(58:14) يَحْلِفُونَ عَلَى الْكَذِبِ they swear to the truthfulness of a falsehood [lit. they swear on the lying].
